commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `AntigravityCLISessionTests` to wait for two manual sleep continuations before resuming teardown in the replacement-launch test, with a comment explaining the idle-timer and grace-period sleeps.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence intermittent reproduction path: current main can satisfy `waitForSleeps(1)` with the lingering idle-timer continuation before the teardown grace continuation parks. The cited CI runs also show failures in the macOS shard that includes this suite, though I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof or maintainer override, not a concrete code repair for ClawSweeper to make.

target.title: fix(test): wait for both sleeps in Antigravity replacement teardown test
target.body:
## Problem
`AntigravityCLISessionTests` intermittently hangs the whole suite to the per-suite **120s timeout** (exit 124) on `swift-test-macos` (e.g. shard 3 on main, runs [28084754287](https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/actions/runs/28084754287) and [28164706265](https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/actions/runs/28164706265)). It is flaky (passes on most runs) and a **timeout, not an assertion failure**; the CI single-suite retry also hangs, so the job fails.

## Root cause (test-only)
The hang is in the test `replacement launch waits for in progress teardown`, not in production.

`AntigravityManualSleeper` (the test fake for `dependencies.sleep`) ignores task cancellation, unlike the real `Task.sleep`:
1. The initial `finishProbe` arms the idle timer (idleWindow 3600 > 0); its Task awaits the sleeper â†’ continuation **S1** registers.
2. `beginProbe("/new/agy")` cancels the idle timer, but the fake ignores cancellation, so **S1 lingers** (in production `Task.sleep` throws on cancel and S1 vanishes).
3. `firstReplacement` enters the teardown grace-period loop (`terminateRootStopsProcess: false`, gracePeriod 1) and awaits the sleeper â†’ continuation **S2** registers, while holding the lifecycle lock.
4. `await waitForSleeps(1)` can be satisfied by **S1 alone**, before S2 registers.
5. `resumeAll()` then resumes only the currently-registered continuations. If S2 hasn't registered yet it is never resumed â†’ the grace loop never exits â†’ the lifecycle lock is never released â†’ the queued second replacement also hangs â†’ 120s timeout. Whether S2 registers before `resumeAll()` is a scheduling race â†’ intermittent (does not reproduce on fast local machines; ~300 local runs + ThreadSanitizer were clean).

## Fix
Wait for **both** sleeps before resuming: `waitForSleeps(1)` â†’ `waitForSleeps(2)`. Both S1 (idle) and S2 (grace) deterministically register, so the count reaches 2 and `resumeAll()` cannot fire before S2 is parked. One line + a comment in the test. **Production `AntigravityCLISession` is unchanged** â€” it is correct; the real `Task.sleep` is cancellation-aware.

## Verification
- Adversarial analysis of every async path (lifecycle mutex, exclusiveProbeWaiters, idle timer, grace loops, ensureStartedLocked) confirmed the production continuations/locks are sound; only this test-fake fidelity gap can strand a continuation.
- The suspect test passed 40/40 in a tight loop with the fix; full `AntigravityCLISessionTests` passes; swiftformat + swiftlint --strict clean.

đź¤– Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
